questions.prompt: Which of the following BEST defines a project?
choices: A routine set of ongoing operational tasks
A temporary endeavour undertaken to create a unique product, service, or result
A long-term strategic plan developed by senior management
A collection of programs and portfolios
questions.explanation: A project is temporary, unique, and constrained by scope, time, and cost.

questions.prompt: In the Triple Constraint model, quality is described as:
choices: The fourth constraint alongside scope, time, and cost
The outcome of how well scope, time, and cost are balanced
A constraint that replaces cost in modern PM frameworks
questions.explanation: Quality is NOT a fourth constraint. It is the outcome — the result of how well scope, time, and cost are managed.

questions.prompt: Which stage of the project life cycle has the HIGHEST level of effort and resource consumption?
questions.explanation: The Executing stage is the longest and most resource-intensive.

questions.prompt: A company manages a group of related IT projects together to deliver a strategic digital transformation. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: A program is a group of related projects managed together as a coordinated whole.

questions.prompt: During which stage is the cost to fix a risk event LOWEST?
questions.explanation: Early in the project, the cost to fix risks is LOW even though likelihood is HIGH.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of a project?
choices: It has a defined start and end date
It involves ongoing and repetitive activities
It has specific time, cost, and performance requirements
questions.explanation: Ongoing and repetitive activities describe operations, not projects.

questions.prompt: The Project Life Cycle consists of which four sequential stages?
questions.explanation: The four stages are Defining, Planning, Executing, and Closing.

questions.prompt: A project sponsor requests additional features midway through execution without extending the deadline. Which constraint is MOST immediately affected?
questions.explanation: Adding features without adjusting the timeline directly increases scope.

questions.prompt: Which stage of the project life cycle is described as the SHORTEST with the LOWEST effort?
questions.explanation: Defining is the shortest stage with the lowest effort.

questions.prompt: What is the PRIMARY difference between a project and an operation?
choices: Projects are temporary and produce unique outputs; operations are ongoing and repetitive
Projects require more staff than operations
Operations involve more risk than projects
questions.explanation: Projects are temporary with defined start/end, while operations are ongoing and repetitive.

questions.prompt: A portfolio in project management is BEST described as:
choices: A group of related projects managed together for strategic benefit
A single large project broken into phases
A collection of programs and/or projects within one organisation, which may be unrelated
The complete documentation of a single project
questions.explanation: A portfolio contains programs and/or projects that may be unrelated.

questions.prompt: If a client demands faster delivery with the same scope and budget, which aspect of the Triple Constraint is MOST likely to suffer?
choices: Quality is most likely to be compromised
The project will be automatically cancelled
questions.explanation: Squeezing time without adjusting scope or cost forces quality trade-offs.

questions.prompt: Risk likelihood is HIGHEST and the cost to fix problems is LOWEST during which project phase?
questions.explanation: Early risk identification is critical because changes are inexpensive early on.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is an example of a project?
choices: A bank processing daily ATM transactions
A hospital conducting annual staff reviews
Launching a new mobile banking app for the first time
Providing ongoing customer service via a call centre
questions.explanation: Launching a new app is temporary, unique, and constrained — all characteristics of a project.

questions.prompt: In the context of project management, 'scope' refers to:
choices: The total budget allocated to the project
The time taken to complete the project
The work that must be done to deliver the project's output
The number of team members assigned
questions.explanation: Scope defines all the work that must be done to deliver the agreed deliverables.
